ai_only_rows.source_quote: In view of the substantial amount of management time that would be required for management presentations, the Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process. The Transaction Committee authorized representatives of GHF to schedule in-person management presentations with the remaining seven potential buyers and allow these parties to conduct additional due diligence.
ai_only_rows.additional_note: one of two lowest IOI bidders excluded by Transaction Committee on 2016-06-01

flags.reason: Filing states two low IOI bidders excluded by Transaction Committee; emitting 2 placeholder DropBelowInf rows (§E5)

ai_only_rows.source_quote: In view of the substantial amount of management time that would be required for management presentations, the Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process. The Transaction Committee authorized representatives of GHF to schedule in-person management presentations with the remaining seven potential buyers and allow these parties to conduct additional due diligence.
ai_only_rows.additional_note: one of two lowest IOI bidders excluded by Transaction Committee on 2016-06-01

ai_only_rows.source_quote: The Board determined that the G&W offer represented the superior proposal. At the request of the Board, representatives of BMO reviewed BMO’s financial analyses supporting its opinion to the Board as to the fairness, from a financial point of view, to the holders of our common stock of the consideration to be received by those holders (other than the Eder Trusts) in the merger pursuant to the merger agreement as of August 12, 2016.
ai_only_rows.additional_note: Board determined G&W offer represented superior proposal after Party B declined to increase. No publicly-announced final-round deadline; target unilaterally closed bidding.

alex_only_rows.comments: In view of the substantial amount of management time that would be required for management presentations, the Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process. The Transaction Committee authorized representatives of GHF to schedule in-person management presentations with the remaining seven potential buyers and allow these parties to conduct additional due diligence. These potential buyers were given access to an internet data site to conduct legal and financial due diligence.
The deadline apparently was not announced to the bidders, this was the time when the English auction was stopped by the target

ai_rows.source_quote: During the week of March 28, 2016, in accordance with the Transaction Committee’s directives, representatives of GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers (including Party A) and 18 potential financial buyers. Each of the potential strategic buyers and 14 potential financial buyers subsequently executed confidentiality agreements.

In early July 2016, a potential strategic buyer that had not previously been part of the process (“Party C”) approached the Company’s management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company. After executing a confidentiality agreement, Party C was provided the memorandum concerning the Company provided to the other potential buyers.
ai_rows.additional_note: Party C was NOT among the 25 initial NDA signers of Mar 28; joined later

alex_rows.comments: Early july 2016 - what should be the appropriate date? July 1?

ai_rows.source_quote: the Board met in executive session (without Mr. Eder present) with representatives of GHF and discussed the status of the process, including that each potential buyer had been advised to submit a non-binding indication of interest by May 10, 2016 (which deadline was subsequently postponed to May 19, 2016 to ensure the potential buyers had sufficient time to formulate their most compelling offers).
ai_rows.additional_note: Initial IOI deadline May 10, 2016 postponed to May 19, 2016. Transaction Committee communicated informal round deadline to NDA signers.

flags.reason: no explicit 'final round' phrase; inferred from subset-invitation-to-submit language per §K2

ai_rows.source_quote: In mid-June 2016, at the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (“LOIs”) by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of the forms of merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement, which GHF indicated would be posted to the internet data site.
ai_rows.additional_note: GHF instructed all NDA signers to submit non-binding LOIs by July 20, 2016 with markups of merger agreement, disclosure letter, and voting agreement

flags.reason: no explicit 'final round' phrase; inferred from process-letter-like instruction to submit LOIs by deadline

alex_rows.comments: Mid-june 2016 | This is probably something we will not collect, but for now…

ai_rows.source_quote: Between May 19, 2016 and June 1, 2016, the Company received nine written indications of interest (“IOIs”) from potential buyers, with offer prices per share ranging from $17.93 to $26.50 (assuming the conversion of preferred stock into common stock), implying equity values of $90 million to $134 million.
ai_rows.additional_note: one of nine written IOIs; aggregate price range $17.93-$26.50 per share across all nine

flags.reason: Filing gives aggregate IOI range $17.93-$26.50 across 9 bids but not per-bidder prices
Filing states 9 written IOIs received; emitting 9 placeholder Bid rows (§E5 exact-count rule)

ai_rows.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: filing calls these 'non-binding indications of interest'; also Final Round Inf Ann process position

ai_rows.source_quote: On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share and Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share. Both revised proposals contained a 30-day due diligence period.
ai_rows.additional_note: Reengagement revised LOI; financing support from Party F; 30-day diligence. Withdrawn on Aug 2.
ai_rows.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: post-LOI revised non-binding proposal; no new markup signal narrated

flags.reason: Party E previously dropped at July 27 Transaction Committee cut; re-engaged July 29, submitted revised LOI Aug 1 with Party F financing

alex_rows.comments: Reengaged | 30 day DD; Financing support from Party F

ai_rows.source_quote: Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share) and Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested that the Company commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during its 30-day due diligence period. In light of the active interest of Party B and G&W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.
ai_rows.additional_note: Withdrew revised $23.81 proposal; filing notes original $21.26 LOI still stood at this moment though target later prioritized G&W and Party B

alex_rows.comments: Did not engage for a while

source_quote: Shortly thereafter, Hinckley Allen and Simpson Thacher finalized the transaction documents and the Company and G&W executed the merger agreement, and the Company, G&W and the Eder Trusts executed the voting agreement. The Company issued a press release publicly announcing the transaction on Monday, August 15, 2016 prior to the opening of trading of the Company’s common stock on the NASDAQ.

At the effective time of the merger, each share of common stock issued and outstanding immediately prior to the effective time of the merger (including any preferred stock deemed to be converted into common stock, but excluding shares that are owned by G&W or any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries, including Merger Sub) will be canceled and will be converted automatically into the right to receive $25.00 in cash, without interest.

Hinckley Allen advised the Board that both Party B’s and G&W’s draft merger agreements restricted the Company from soliciting a competing proposal, subject to a “fiduciary out” for an unsolicited superior proposal, which required the Company to pay a termination fee equal to 3% of the transaction value in the case of G&W (which the Company had negotiated down from 3.85%) and 3% plus Party B’s transaction expenses (capped at 1% of transaction value) in the case of Party B, in the event the merger agreement was terminated as a result of the Board’s decision to pursue such a proposal.
ai_rows.additional_note: Merger agreement executed Aug 12, 2016; public announcement Aug 15, 2016. $25.00/share in cash. Termination fee 3% of transaction value.

alex_rows.comments: Confirm 7/20/2016 bid after DD | Restrict from soliciting competing bids

ai_rows.source_quote: Between May 19, 2016 and June 1, 2016, the Company received nine written indications of interest (“IOIs”) from potential buyers, with offer prices per share ranging from $17.93 to $26.50 (assuming the conversion of preferred stock into common stock), implying equity values of $90 million to $134 million.
ai_rows.additional_note: one of nine written IOIs; aggregate price range $17.93-$26.50 per share across all nine

ai_rows.source_quote: On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share and Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share. Both revised proposals contained a 30-day due diligence period.
ai_rows.additional_note: Reengagement revised LOI; financing support from Party F; 30-day diligence. Withdrawn on Aug 2.
ai_rows.bid_type_inference_note: informal per §G1: post-LOI revised non-binding proposal; no new markup signal narrated

flags.reason: Party E previously dropped at July 27 Transaction Committee cut; re-engaged July 29, submitted revised LOI Aug 1 with Party F financing

alex_rows.comments: Reengaged | 30 day DD; Financing support from Party F

ai_rows.source_quote: Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share) and Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested that the Company commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during its 30-day due diligence period. In light of the active interest of Party B and G&W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.
ai_rows.additional_note: Withdrew revised $23.81 proposal; filing notes original $21.26 LOI still stood at this moment though target later prioritized G&W and Party B

alex_rows.comments: Did not engage for a while

source_quote: Shortly thereafter, Hinckley Allen and Simpson Thacher finalized the transaction documents and the Company and G&W executed the merger agreement, and the Company, G&W and the Eder Trusts executed the voting agreement. The Company issued a press release publicly announcing the transaction on Monday, August 15, 2016 prior to the opening of trading of the Company’s common stock on the NASDAQ.

At the effective time of the merger, each share of common stock issued and outstanding immediately prior to the effective time of the merger (including any preferred stock deemed to be converted into common stock, but excluding shares that are owned by G&W or any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries, including Merger Sub) will be canceled and will be converted automatically into the right to receive $25.00 in cash, without interest.

Hinckley Allen advised the Board that both Party B’s and G&W’s draft merger agreements restricted the Company from soliciting a competing proposal, subject to a “fiduciary out” for an unsolicited superior proposal, which required the Company to pay a termination fee equal to 3% of the transaction value in the case of G&W (which the Company had negotiated down from 3.85%) and 3% plus Party B’s transaction expenses (capped at 1% of transaction value) in the case of Party B, in the event the merger agreement was terminated as a result of the Board’s decision to pursue such a proposal.
ai_rows.additional_note: Merger agreement executed Aug 12, 2016; public announcement Aug 15, 2016. $25.00/share in cash. Termination fee 3% of transaction value.

alex_rows.comments: Confirm 7/20/2016 bid after DD | Restrict from soliciting competing bids
